Empires & Puzzles Kai Review: The Very Fast Plains Hunter Sniper

Kai burst onto the scene with Very Fast mana and annoying bone traps, but how does this Plains Hunter fare against the gods and monsters of today? We analyze if his speed can outrun his aging stats in this comprehensive review.
Kai: The Mantis Striker - Speed Kills (But Only If It Hits)
Kai, The Mantis Striker: Speed Kills (But Only If It Hits Hard Enough)

Kai is a 5-star Legendary Nature (Green) hero from the Plains Hunter family (originally introduced as the Monster Hunter family). Released back in late 2022, he fits the classic archetype of a "Glass Cannon Sniper"—designed to charge quickly, hit a single target, and leave a nasty parting gift. His main selling points are his
mana speed and his ability to summon a

that not only damages but also cripples healing. While his gender has been a topic of debate in the community (spoiler: the game generally treats him as male, though the art is beautifully androgynous), there's no debating his role: he exists to hunt down and neutralize key threats before they can breathe.

Kai sports a vibrant, tribal aesthetic consistent with the Plains Hunter theme. Adorned in green armor with organic, insect-like plating (fitting the "Mantis" theme) and a striking headdress, he looks like he leaped straight out of a prehistoric jungle. The art is dynamic, holding a spear that looks ready to pierce through a Titan's hide. It’s a refreshing break from the standard "knight in shining armor" trope, leaning heavily into a wild, agile hunter vibe. He looks fast, which is perfect because... well, he is.
Here is the hard truth: Kai's stats are significantly outdated.
Modern heroes (like those from the Astral or Construct families) are pushing
base power. Kai is nearly 400 points behind.
While 889 was respectable in 2022, modern damage dealers often boast
attack stats. This directly impacts his damage output, making his "snipe" feel more like a "slap" against modern defenses.

His bulk (832 Def / 1569 HP) is paper-thin by today's standards (where 1300 Def / 2200 HP is the norm). He will struggle to survive even a single hit from a modern meta hero.
Even with Limit Breaks (LB2), his stats only reach roughly where modern heroes start

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Plains Hunter family bonus provides an Attack boost for
heroes on the team. It's a nice passive stat bump, but given how rare these heroes are (and how few you'd run together nowadays), it's rarely a game-changer.

Applies a DoT (150 dmg over 3 turns) to a random enemy when he fires. It's negligible damage in today's meta (50 damage/turn is a scratch), but it can occasionally pop a fighter revive or finish a low-hp enemy.

. This is actually quite useful in specific wars (like against the Three Kingdoms family), preventing him from being burdened by enemy fiends. It adds a layer of survivability, but doesn't fix his low stats.
Upon release, Kai was considered a "must-have" for his speed and anti-heal capabilities. He was a menace in Rush wars and a solid flank in standard defenses.

The game has moved on. His damage output is now comparable to a modern 4-star hero. His fiend mechanics remain annoying, but his inability to survive the massive AoE hits of 2026 makes him a liability in top-tier play.
He is no longer viable in top-tier Diamond raids or Alliance Wars as a primary attacker. He simply lacks the firepower to dent 3000+ HP tanks. However, he remains useful for:
His speed allows him to finish off wounded targets quickly.
If you are a newer player, he is still an excellent sniper who will serve you well until you face the latest monsters.
, which gives him a chance to dodge direct damage. This pairs excellently with his squishy nature.
Maximizes his "Mantis Strike" damage and the fiend's attack stat. He needs every point of attack to stay relevant.
This is the only viable path. If Kai isn't killing or severely maiming his target, he's useless. Go full attack (Sword path).
path is recommended. You play Kai for speed and damage; trying to make him tanky is a lost cause.
Charging in 6 tiles makes him one of the fastest dispellers/snipers in the game.
The combination of a healing-absorbing fiend AND a -50% healing generation debuff is devastating against healer-heavy teams.
Removing buffs at the end of the turn for 3 turns can disrupt teams that rely on recurring buffs or HoT (Heal over Time).
His damage and survivability are critically low compared to modern heroes.
In a meta dominated by "Hit All" nukes, a single-target sniper feels low-impact.
His survival often depends on a 10% passive proc or a Rogue dodge.
Paradoxically, while his anti-heal is useless here (no healing allowed anyway), his speed and raw damage (if emblems are high) make him a decent sniper.
Perfect for taking out that one annoying corner healer left over from a failed attack.
Bringing him against a heavy healer tank (like Hathor or Cleaver) can shut them down if you can keep him alive.
Use an Elemental Defense Down hero first. Kai's damage is low, but with Green Defense Down, he hits respectable numbers. His speed allows him to keep pace with fast EDD heroes.
Kai - a hero with Heal Block (e.g., Perkin, costumed Sand Empire)
Layering healing ailments ensures the enemy stays low.
Do not pair him with other fiend summoners if you are facing a hero like
, who punish you for having too many fiends.

If you pulled Kai and you are in Gold or Platinum arena, rejoice! He is a fantastic sniper who will teach you the value of mana control and speed. Level him up, put him on the wing of your defense, or use him to snipe key targets in raids. He will carry you far until you hit the Diamond wall.
Kai is likely a "museum piece" for you. Unless you desperately need a green hero for
, he is not worth the tonics or Alpha Aethers. His stats are just too far behind to justify the resources compared to a modern Toon Costume or Construct hero.
The community has always had a soft spot for Kai's design, even if his performance was debated.
"Is that a man or a woman... I can't tell."
The consensus settled on "Hunter Knight" generally being male, but the ambiguity remains part of the charm.
Upon release, polls showed split opinions. While many voted "Yes, absolutely," a significant chunk said "Not really" or "Only if P2P." Time has proven the skeptics right regarding his longevity.
"I use Khonshu... not sure if I would pull for Kai now."
Khonshu aged slightly better due to his mana-gain-on-kill mechanic allowing for chain kills, which Kai lacks.
Kai is a Ferrari engine in a go-kart chassis. The
—Very Fast speed, healing suppression, and dispel—are excellent and timeless. However, the
—his attack and defense—are from a bygone era. He is a fun, tactical hero who can still win games in the mid-tiers, but don't expect him to hunt down the giants of the modern era.
A solid B-tier hero for depth, but keep your Alpha Aethers for someone who hits like a truck, not a mantis.
Is Kai still worth ascending in 2026? A deep dive into the 5-star Nature sniper from the Plains Hunter family, analyzing his Very Fast speed, Fiend mechanics, and viability in the current meta.
